title: Farm Workforce Support Act of 2025
plain_summary: This bill tells the government's money watchdog to study a program that brings foreign workers to American farms for temporary jobs. The study must look at how this program affects things like pay, housing, and working conditions for both American workers and foreign workers. It also needs to check how the program impacts families' money situations and whether farms are hiring differently because of it. The watchdog has two years to finish the study and tell Congress what they found.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on the Judiciary.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Farm Workforce Support Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2 Report on H-2A Temporary Visa Program
sections.text: The Comptroller General (the top government official who checks how the government spends money) must write a report about the H-2A program. The report must be finished and sent to certain committees in Congress within two years after this law passes. The report must include five main parts. First, it must study how the program affects hiring practices, including how it impacts American workers and guest workers from other countries, and whether American companies are using the program more and more. Second, it must identify problems with finding enough affordable housing for guest workers. Third, it must look at how the wage requirements in the program affect whether American companies can find American workers versus guest workers. Fourth, it must analyze the economic impact when guest workers cannot bring their spouses and unmarried children to work with them and earn money. Fifth, it must examine whether employers are following the work condition promises they make in contracts with guest workers under the H-2A program. This section also defines important terms used in the law. An American employer means any person, company, or organization in the United States that hires someone and pays them wages or salary. Certain Congressional committees means four specific committees: the Committee on Education and the Workforce in the House of Representatives, the Committee on Agriculture in the House of Representatives, the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions in the Senate, and the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry in the Senate. A domestic worker means a United States citizen who works in the United States. A guest worker means a worker from another country who comes to the United States temporarily under a specific part of immigration law. The H-2A program means the system that allows these temporary foreign workers to come work in American agriculture.
